mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive during months with positive PAYEMS growth (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% reflects restrictive monetary conditions that raise discount rates and depress earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising consumer prices and falling industrial production identify a stagflationary supply shock that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and INDPRO yields a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of stagflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns during post-recession recovery periods as capacity utilization rebuilds, and this predictive power disappears outside of recovery regimes.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO growth are not significantly higher in post-recession periods than in other regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes negative earnings revisions and equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Joint accelerations in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based domestic demand momentum that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of RSAFS and HOUST produces a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-acceleration months.

mechanism_narrative: A declining trend in consumer price inflation reduces real discount rates and supports equity multiple expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns during months with declining CPIAUCSL are not significantly positive compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns during early-cycle recovery phases when credit conditions are easing, and this predictive power disappears during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following positive HOUST changes are not significantly higher during early-cycle recovery with easing credit than during tightening cycles (t-test p > 0.05).
